attractions.name: Baga Beach
attractions.description: One of North Goa's busiest beaches, lined with shacks, water sports operators, and a lively promenade. Popular for parasailing, jet-skiing, and banana boat rides by day.

attractions.name: Calangute Beach
attractions.description: The largest and most crowded beach in North Goa, known as the 'Queen of Beaches', with a long stretch of sand, shacks, and markets nearby.
attractions.transport_tips: Well connected by local buses and taxis

attractions.name: Fort Aguada
attractions.description: A well-preserved 17th-century Portuguese fort and lighthouse overlooking the Arabian Sea at Sinquerim, built to defend against Dutch and Maratha naval attacks.
attractions.transport_tips: Combine with a Sinquerim/Candolim beach visit

attractions.name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site in Old Goa holding the mortal remains of St. Francis Xavier. One of the finest examples of baroque architecture in India.
attractions.transport_tips: Located in Old Goa, 10km from Panaji

attractions.name: Se Cathedral
attractions.description: One of the largest churches in Asia, built by the Portuguese in Old Goa, famous for its Golden Bell — one of the best bells in the world.
attractions.transport_tips: Walking distance from Basilica of Bom Jesus

attractions.name: Chapora Fort
attractions.description: A hilltop laterite fort overlooking Vagator beach and the Chapora river mouth, popular for panoramic sunset views and made famous by the Bollywood film 'Dil Chahta Hai'.
attractions.transport_tips: Short uphill walk from Vagator village

attractions.name: Dudhsagar Falls
attractions.description: A dramatic four-tiered waterfall on the Mandovi river near the Goa-Karnataka border, reached via a jeep safari through Bhagwan Mahavir Wildlife Sanctuary from Mollem.
attractions.transport_tips: Book a shared or private jeep safari from Mollem

attractions.name: Anjuna Flea Market
attractions.description: A legendary Wednesday flea market since the hippie era of the 1960s, selling textiles, jewellery, souvenirs, and Goan handicrafts.

attractions.name: Mangeshi Temple
attractions.description: One of Goa's oldest and most revered Hindu temples, dedicated to Lord Manguesh (a form of Shiva), located in Priol, Ponda.
attractions.transport_tips: Best visited by car/taxi from Panaji, ~25 min

attractions.name: Palolem Beach
attractions.description: A calm, crescent-shaped South Goa beach fringed with palm trees, far quieter than North Goa's beaches, popular for kayaking and dolphin-spotting boat trips.

attractions.name: Fontainhas (Latin Quarter)
attractions.description: Panaji's old Latin Quarter, a maze of narrow lanes lined with pastel-painted Portuguese-era houses, art galleries, and cafes.

restaurants.name: Fisherman's Wharf, Cavelossim
restaurants.description: Riverside restaurant known for authentic Goan seafood thalis, prawn curry, and live crab preparations.

restaurants.name: Vinayak Family Restaurant, Assagao
restaurants.description: A local favourite for Goan fish thali and home-style curries, popular with both tourists and locals.

restaurants.name: Gunpowder, Assagao
restaurants.description: A leafy, garden-set restaurant specialising in South Indian and Kerala-style dishes alongside Goan specials.

restaurants.name: Curlies Beach Shack, Anjuna
restaurants.description: An iconic beach shack on Anjuna beach serving seafood and cocktails with live music evenings.

hidden_gems.name: Grande Island
hidden_gems.description: A lesser-known island off Bogmalo, popular with divers for scuba diving trips to see coral and reef fish — far less crowded than the main beaches.

hidden_gems.name: Divar Island
hidden_gems.description: A peaceful river island reached by a short government ferry from Old Goa, dotted with Portuguese-era villages, paddy fields, and quiet churches — almost no tourist crowds.

hidden_gems.name: Arpora Saturday Night Market
hidden_gems.description: A lively weekly night bazaar (also called Ingo's Night Market) with live music stages, international food stalls, and boutique stalls — a more atmospheric alternative to Anjuna's daytime market.

nightlife.name: Tito's Lane, Baga
nightlife.description: Goa's best-known nightlife strip packed with clubs, bars, and lounges, historically anchored by the long-running Tito's nightclub.

nightlife.name: Mandovi River Casino Cruises
nightlife.description: Floating casino boats docked on the Mandovi river off Panaji, offering gaming tables, slot machines, and buffet dinners.

shopping.name: Mapusa Municipal Market
shopping.description: A bustling local market for spices, cashews, feni (local cashew liquor), and everyday Goan produce, popular with locals rather than tourists.

festivals.name: Goa Carnival
festivals.description: A four-day pre-Lent street carnival with parades, floats, music, and dancing across Goa's main towns, led by the legendary King Momo.
festivals.best_time: February/March (dates vary with the Christian calendar)

festivals.name: Sunburn Festival
festivals.description: One of Asia's largest electronic dance music festivals, held on Candolim/Vagator beach grounds, drawing international DJs.

adventure_activities.name: Parasailing at Baga/Calangute
adventure_activities.description: Beach-launched parasailing rides offering aerial views of the North Goa coastline.

adventure_activities.name: Bhagwan Mahavir Wildlife Sanctuary Trek
adventure_activities.description: Forest trails near Mollem through the Western Ghats, home to the Dudhsagar falls trail and diverse birdlife.

local_tips: Rent a scooter for the most flexible way to explore North Goa's beaches; an international driving permit or valid Indian license is required.
North Goa (Baga, Calangute, Anjuna) is livelier and nightlife-focused; South Goa (Palolem, Agonda) is quieter and more upscale/relaxed.
Beach shacks are seasonal — most operate October to May and close during the monsoon (June-September).
Carry cash for small vendors and local markets; card acceptance is inconsistent outside main resorts.

safety.general: Goa is generally safe for tourists including solo and female travellers, but exercise standard caution at nightlife venues and avoid swimming during red-flag warnings — rip currents cause fatalities most years.
safety.water_safety: Swim only at beaches with lifeguards on duty and heed flag warnings; the Arabian Sea undertow can be strong, especially in monsoon season.
safety.transport_safety: Wear a helmet when renting a scooter; traffic enforcement and road conditions vary.

culture.overview: Goa blends Hindu and Portuguese-Catholic heritage — expect a mix of temple traditions and centuries-old church architecture, plus a laid-back 'susegad' (relaxed) way of life.
culture.etiquette: Dress modestly when visiting temples and churches (cover shoulders/knees); beachwear is fine on the beach itself but not in town centres.

transport.getting_around: Scooters and taxis are the main way to get around; there's no widespread metro or ride-hailing coverage outside a few apps in Panaji. Pre-paid taxi counters exist at the airport.
transport.airport: Goa is served by Dabolim Airport (GOI) near Vasco da Gama and the newer Manohar International Airport (GOX) at Mopa in North Goa.

visa_information.domestic_indian_travellers: No visa needed for Indian citizens — a valid government photo ID is sufficient for hotel check-in.
visa_information.foreign_nationals: Most foreign visitors need a valid Indian visa (e-Visa available for many nationalities) obtained before arrival.

weather.avoid: June to September (heavy monsoon rains, rough seas, many shacks closed)
